situation: The launch-week state is compiled, but new studio context (post-launch commitments, v1.1 ideas, life constraints) has not been captured since 2026-06-30.
recommended_path: Paste rough bullets into the intake scratchpad, then run the proposal command; everything lands as reviewable packets, never direct writes.
exact_ask: Paste any new projects or commitments between the intake markers, then run the proposal command for the active-projects scope.
review_policy: The intake pipeline does not write state or memory. It prints a draft-for-approval packet; only an explicit operator approval promotes anything.

options.label: Active Projects
options.why: Projects and commitments are the backbone of the map; capturing them first makes every other surface useful.
options.send_format: - project name - why it matters - status - next action - deadline - blocker
options.example_prompt: - Skylark v1.1 export feature - top survey request - planned - next: scope month export - after launch retro

options.label: Daily Routine
options.why: The launch-week cadence is captured, but the post-launch rhythm is not; routines keep the assistant's suggestions honest.
options.send_format: - routine name - cadence - steps - what usually derails it - what to enforce
options.example_prompt: - Post-launch review inbox - daily - read store reviews, tag themes - derailed by feature work - enforce 20-minute cap

options.label: Life Areas
options.why: Life-area context is the most sensitive layer; it only enters memory through high-risk review packets.
options.send_format: - area - what it is responsible for - current pain - desired standard - anything gated
options.example_prompt: - Health - keep climbing twice a week through launch - currently skipped - standard: never two misses in a row
